title: Bilibili Unveils Open-Source Active AI Companion &\#x27;Project N.E.K.O.&\#x27; at WAIC 2026
summary: Bilibili showcased its open-source AI companion &\#x27;Project N.E.K.O.&\#x27; at WAIC 2026, an active multimodal system that understands desktop content and initiates conversations. This represents a significant shift from passive AI assistants to proactive AI companions that perceive and interact with the user&\#x27;s digital environment, potentially transforming how users engage with virtual beings. The system features a separated architecture for UI, AI brain, and memory, allowing core data to remain local. It supports Live2D and VRM avatars, physical feedback, TTS with voice cloning, and multilingual capabilities.
context: AI companions are virtual beings designed for emotional support and interaction. Traditional AI assistants are reactive, responding only when called. Project N.E.K.O. is &\#x27;active&\#x27;, meaning it continuously perceives the user&\#x27;s screen and environment, initiating conversations based on context. VRM is an open 3D avatar format standard for VR and VTubing, while VoiceClone uses AI to replicate a person&\#x27;s voice from audio samples.
